subject: Added vpci and vsonh tests
commit_message:
Added vpci and vsonh tests

Added test cases for vpci and vsonh usecases

Issue-ID: POLICY-2162
Change-Id: Ia69b16e74b82b92366b5065eeccab8d35f647742
Signed-off-by: pramod.jamkhedkar <pramod@research.att.com>
Signed-off-by: Jim Hahn <jrh3@att.com>
Signed-off-by: pramod.jamkhedkar <pramod@research.att.com>

Patch Set 6:

Are there tests for the equivalent policy as a tosca compliant policy (I only see legacy).
It would be good to make sure that they also work.

Patch Set 6:

> Patch Set 6:
> 
> Are there tests for the equivalent policy as a tosca compliant policy (I only see legacy).
> It would be good to make sure that they also work.

I had asked this question a couple of weeks back to vPCI/VSon team and the response I received was:  
"I guess, both vpci and vson control loops are being created using old policy formats. We are cretiang and pushing this policy into the drools for the usecase. PFA the control loop policies."

Patch Set 6:

> Patch Set 6:
> 
> > Patch Set 6:
> > 
> > Are there tests for the equivalent policy as a tosca compliant policy (I only see legacy).
> > It would be good to make sure that they also work.
> 
> I had asked this question a couple of weeks back to vPCI/VSon team and the response I received was:  
> "I guess, both vpci and vson control loops are being created using old policy formats. We are cretiang and pushing this policy into the drools for the usecase. PFA the control loop policies."

They should NOT be using the legacy policies, there is no reason for them to stay with legacy policy syntax. That was made clear to their team from the start of Frankfurt planning that they are dependent on the actor cleanup and will require the use of the new TOSCA compliant policy types.

Patch Set 6:

> Patch Set 6:
> 
> > Patch Set 6:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 6:
> > > 
> > > Are there tests for the equivalent policy as a tosca compliant policy (I only see legacy).
> > > It would be good to make sure that they also work.
> > 
> > I had asked this question a couple of weeks back to vPCI/VSon team and the response I received was:  
> > "I guess, both vpci and vson control loops are being created using old policy formats. We are cretiang and pushing this policy into the drools for the usecase. PFA the control loop policies."
> 
> They should NOT be using the legacy policies, there is no reason for them to stay with legacy policy syntax. That was made clear to their team from the start of Frankfurt planning that they are dependent on the actor cleanup and will require the use of the new TOSCA compliant policy types.

Let me ask them again. I have a call with them tomorrow morning.

Patch Set 6: -Code-Review

> > They should NOT be using the legacy policies, there is no reason for them to stay with legacy policy syntax. That was made clear to their team from the start of Frankfurt planning that they are dependent on the actor cleanup and will require the use of the new TOSCA compliant policy types.
> 

should be easy enough to create a compliant policy for our testing.  Can create it from scratch, or by URL-decoding what's in the legacy policy.

>

> > 
> 
> should be easy enough to create a compliant policy for our testing.  Can create it from scratch, or by URL-decoding what's in the legacy policy.

Yes it should be very easy to - it maps pretty easily.
